title: tonsella elliptica
intro: Tonsella elliptica, also known as the Red-belted Polypore, is a species of bracket fungus that grows on trees, typically found in North America. This fungus has been utilized for its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, making it a potential treatment for infections and wounds. The health benefits of Tonsella elliptica include its potential to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Tonsella elliptica is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
